title: Swallowed Poison
symptoms: Burns or redness around the mouth and lips
immediate_steps: If the person is unconscious, having seizures, or not breathing, call emergency services immediately
If conscious and stable, call Poison Control
Find out what was taken, how much, and when
Wipe the mouth clean and remove any remaining poison
Keep the person calm and monitor breathing
avoid: Do not induce vomiting unless explicitly told to do so by Poison Control (vomiting chemicals can cause double damage)
Do not give milk, water, or activated charcoal unless instructed
Do not wait for symptoms to appear before calling for help

title: Inhaled Poison (Carbon Monoxide, Chemicals)
symptoms: Cherry red skin (rare, in late CO poisoning)
immediate_steps: Get the person out of the toxic environment into fresh air immediately
Call emergency services
If the person is not breathing, begin CPR
Open doors and windows to ventilate the area if it's safe to do so
avoid: Do not enter a toxic environment without proper breathing apparatus (you might become a victim too)
Do not light a match or use electronics if a gas leak is suspected

title: Poison on the Skin
immediate_steps: Remove contaminated clothing using gloves
Brush off dry powders from the skin
Rinse the affected skin with a continuous stream of water for at least 15-20 minutes
Wash gently with soap and water
Call Poison Control or emergency services
avoid: Do not touch the poison with your bare hands
Do not apply ointments or creams without medical advice
Do not scrub the skin harshly

title: Poisoning - Identification and Immediate Action
immediate_steps: Immediately remove the person from exposure if it is safe to do so
Check responsiveness and breathing
Call emergency services without delay
Try to identify the poison if possible by checking containers or surroundings
avoid: Do not wait for symptoms to worsen before calling for help
Do not waste time trying to identify the poison if it delays treatment

title: Poisoning - Do's and Don'ts
immediate_steps: Do call emergency services immediately and provide information about the suspected poison
Do keep the person calm and monitor breathing and consciousness
Do remove contaminated clothing if the poison is on the skin and rinse with clean water
avoid: Do not induce vomiting unless instructed by medical professionals
Do not give food, milk, or water unless advised by emergency services
Do not attempt home remedies or delay hospital treatment

title: Poisoning - Types of Poison Exposure
immediate_steps: Ingestion: toxic substances swallowed (chemicals, medicines, spoiled food)
Inhalation: toxic gases or fumes breathed in (carbon monoxide, smoke)
Skin contact: harmful chemicals absorbed through the skin
Injection: toxins enter through bites, stings, or contaminated needles
Seek immediate medical attention as each type requires different first aid precautions

title: Poisoning - Stabilization and Transport
immediate_steps: Keep the person lying down and monitor breathing, pulse, and consciousness continuously
If vomiting occurs, turn the person on their side to prevent choking
Bring any container or sample of the suspected poison to assist medical treatment
Transport to a hospital immediately
avoid: Do not give anything by mouth unless instructed by medical professionals
